-- Parse index.keyword or just keyword local index, keyword = 1, args if args == "" then -- No args: use current target local target = State.get_target() if not target or not target.keyword or target.keyword == "" then Util.error("Quick where: no target. Use xcp to select a target first.") return end keyword = target.keyword else -- Check for N.keyword format local n, kw = string.match(args, "^(%d+)%.(.+)$") if n then index = tonumber(n) keyword = kw end end
-- Manual qw: exclusive â cancel any other hunting tool HuntTrick.reset() AutoHunt.reset() QuickWhere.start(index, keyword) end
--- Quick scan / smart scan command (Phase 4 / Item A5) -- Behavior matches Crowley's quick_scan + smart_scan (Search_and_Destroy.xml:3828, 3837): -- * On CP/GQ with target â smart-scan: SendNoEcho("scan"), full nearby scan, -- auto-fallback to `con` if some mobs were noscan/hidden -- * Has target but not on CP/GQ â quick-scan: Send("scan <keyword>"), filtered -- * No target â Send("scan"), plain -- Tested by: cmd_qs.smart_scan_when_on_activity, cmd_qs.filtered_when_target_no_activity, -- cmd_qs.plain_when_no_target function cmd_qs(name, line, wildcards) local target = State.get_target() local activity = State.get_activity() local on_activity = (activity == "cp" or activity == "gq")
if target and on_activity then -- Smart-scan path: full scan + auto-con fallback for hidden mobs SmartScan._running_smart_scan = true SendNoEcho("scan") elseif target and target.keyword and target.keyword ~= "" then -- Quick-scan path: filtered scan for the target only Send("scan " .. target.keyword) else -- No target: plain scan Send("scan") end end
--- Auto-hunt command handler -- Usage: ah (use current target), ah <keyword>, ah abort/cancel/0 -- Tested by: cmd_ah.cancel_resets, .abort_resets, .zero_resets, -- .no_target_error, .uses_current_target, .with_keyword_arg function cmd_ah(name, line, wildcards) local args = Util.trim(wildcards[1] or "")
-- Abort commands if args == "abort" or args == "cancel" or args == "0" then AutoHunt.reset() Util.info("Auto-hunt cancelled.") return end
local keyword = args if args == "" then -- No args: use current target local target = State.get_target() if not target or not target.keyword or target.keyword == "" then Util.error("Auto-hunt: no target. Use xcp to select a target first.") return end keyword = target.keyword end
-- Manual ah: exclusive â cancel any other hunting tool HuntTrick.reset() QuickWhere.reset() AutoHunt.start(keyword) end
function cmd_xrt(name, line, wildcards) local args = Util.trim(wildcards[1] or "") if args == "" then Util.error("Usage: xrt <area> â speedwalk to area start room") return end local area_key = Nav.fuzzy_match_area(args) if not area_key then Util.error("Unknown area: ", args) return end Nav.goto_area(area_key) end
function cmd_kk(name, line, wildcards) local kk_cmd = Config.get("quick_kill_command") or "k" local target = State.get_target() if target and target.keyword then SendNoEcho(kk_cmd .. " " .. target.keyword) else Util.error("No target set. Use xcp to select a target first.") end end
------------------------------------------------------------------------ -- Trigger Callbacks (stubs - detailed implementation in later phases) ------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- CP INFO triggers function on_cp_info_level(name, line, wildcards) CP._level = tonumber(wildcards[1]) or 0 Util.debug("CP level: ", tostring(CP._level)) end
--- Callback: "The targets for this campaign are:" â enable line+end triggers -- Side effects: clears _info_list, enables trg_cp_info_line + trg_cp_info_end, -- disables trg_cp_info_start (Crowley's pattern: stage triggers to prevent premature end) -- Tested by: CP.do_info.start_enables_line_and_end, CP.info_parse_flow function on_cp_info_start(name, line, wildcards) CP._info_list = {} EnableTrigger("trg_cp_info_start", false) EnableTrigger("trg_cp_info_line", true) EnableTrigger("trg_cp_info_end", true) end
function on_cp_info_line(name, line, wildcards) CP._info_list[#CP._info_list + 1] = { mob = wildcards[1], location = wildcards[2], } end
--- Callback: cp info parsing complete â set CP active, detect type, chain to cp check -- Side effects: disables all grp_cp_info triggers individually, sets CP._on_cp = true -- + activity = "cp", detects area/room type, schedules CP.do_check() via 0.1s DoAfterSpecial -- Tested by: CP.info_parse_flow (callbacks only), CP.info_end_sets_cp_state, -- workflow.cp_info_to_check_chain, workflow.xcp_picks_up_existing_cp function on_cp_info_end(name, line, wildcards) EnableTrigger("trg_cp_info_level", false) EnableTrigger("trg_cp_info_start", false) EnableTrigger("trg_cp_info_line", false) EnableTrigger("trg_cp_info_end", false) EnableTimer("tim_parse_safety", false)
-- Mark as on-CP (handles mid-campaign plugin load/reconnect) CP._on_cp = true State.set_activity("cp")
-- Detect area vs room type from info list CP._type = TargetList.detect_type(CP._info_list) Util.debug("CP type: ", CP._type, " (", tostring(#CP._info_list), " targets)")
-- Chain to cp check after short delay (allows MUD output to settle) DoAfterSpecial(0.1, "CP.do_check()", sendto.script) end
-- CP CHECK triggers --- Callback: each "You still have to kill *" line â enable end trigger on first match -- Side effects: appends to CP._check_list, enables trg_cp_check_end on first line -- Tested by: CP.check_parse_flow function on_cp_check_line(name, line, wildcards) -- Enable end trigger on first line match (prevents premature firing) if #CP._check_list == 0 then EnableTrigger("trg_cp_check_end", true) end local dead = (wildcards[3] == "Dead") or false CP._check_list[#CP._check_list + 1] = { mob = wildcards[1], location = wildcards[2], dead = dead, } end
--- Callback: cp check parsing complete â build target list, auto-display on cold pickup -- Side effects: disables check triggers individually, sets CP active, builds TargetList, -- displays list when no prev_target (cold mid-CP pickup so user sees what was loaded) -- Tested by: CP.check_parse_flow, workflow.cp_check_builds_target_list, -- workflow.xcp_pickup_displays_list_on_cold_start, workflow.kill_refresh_does_not_display_list function on_cp_check_end(name, line, wildcards) EnableTrigger("trg_cp_check_line", false) EnableTrigger("trg_cp_check_end", false) EnableTimer("tim_parse_safety", false)
-- CP._on_cp and State._activity are already set by CP.start(). -- Re-confirm here defensively (handles reconnect case where cp check -- is sent without cp info, e.g., after plugin reload mid-campaign). CP._on_cp = true State.set_activity("cp")
-- Guard: empty check list means parse failed if #CP._check_list == 0 then Util.error("CP check parse failed (no targets received)") return end
-- Build target list from check data TargetList.build(CP._check_list, CP._type, CP._level)
-- Always re-match current target against new list -- (handles: mob killed refresh, manual refresh, other player killed a mob) local prev_target = CP._last_target or State.get_target() CP._last_target = nil if prev_target then local found = TargetList.find_by_mob(prev_target.mob) if found and not found.dead then -- Prior target is still alive â keep it State.set_target(found) else -- Prior target is dead or gone â auto-select next alive target local alive = TargetList.get_alive() if #alive > 0 then State.set_target(alive[1]) else State.clear_target() end end else -- Cold mid-CP pickup: no prior target to re-match. Show the freshly-built -- list so the user sees what was loaded without typing xcp a second time. TargetList.display() end end
-- CP EVENT triggers function on_cp_request(name, line, wildcards) CP.start() end
function on_cp_mob_killed(name, line, wildcards) if State.get_activity() ~= "cp" then return end
-- Save current target for re-matching after refresh CP._last_target = State.get_target()
Util.info("Campaign mob killed!")
-- Refresh target list after short delay DoAfterSpecial(0.1, "CP.do_check()", sendto.script) end
function on_cp_complete(name, line, wildcards) Util.important("Campaign complete!") CP.clear() end
function on_cp_cleared(name, line, wildcards) Util.info("Campaign cleared.") CP.clear() end
function on_cp_new_available(name, line, wildcards) CP._can_get_new = true Util.debug("New campaign available") end
function on_cp_not_on(name, line, wildcards) if CP._on_cp then CP.clear() end end
function on_cp_must_level(name, line, wildcards) CP._can_get_new = false -- Turn noexp off â player needs to level (TODO: test live at appropriate level) if Noexp._noexp_on then Noexp.set(false) Util.info("Turning noexp ", "OFF", " (must level for new campaign)") end end
function on_cp_timer(name, line, wildcards) -- Superhero CP cooldown (TODO: verify live at superhero level) CP._can_get_new = false end
-- GQ INFO triggers function on_gq_info_quest_name(name, line, wildcards) end function on_gq_info_extended(name, line, wildcards) end function on_gq_info_level(name, line, wildcards) end function on_gq_info_line(name, line, wildcards) end function on_gq_info_end(name, line, wildcards) end
-- GQ CHECK triggers function on_gq_check_line(name, line, wildcards) end function on_gq_check_end(name, line, wildcards) end
-- GQ EVENT triggers function on_gq_mob_killed(name, line, wildcards) end function on_gq_joined(name, line, wildcards) end function on_gq_started(name, line, wildcards) end function on_gq_finished(name, line, wildcards) end function on_gq_ended(name, line, wildcards) end function on_gq_not_started(name, line, wildcards) end function on_gq_won(name, line, wildcards) end function on_gq_cancelled(name, line, wildcards) end function on_gq_quit(name, line, wildcards) end function on_gq_not_in(name, line, wildcards) end
-- HUNT TRICK triggers
--- Direction/portal found: increment index and hunt again -- Tested by: on_ht_direction.increments_and_hunts function on_ht_direction(name, line, wildcards) if not HuntTrick._active then return end HuntTrick._first_target = false local new_index = HuntTrick._index + 1 local keyword = HuntTrick._keyword -- Don't call full start() â just update index, send next hunt HuntTrick._index = new_index Send(string.format("hunt %d.%s", new_index, keyword)) end
--- Mob is here: chain to QW exact to identify the room (skipped when chain disabled) -- Tested by: on_ht_here.chains_to_qw_exact, on_ht_here.no_target_notifies, on_ht_here.no_chain function on_ht_here(name, line, wildcards) if not HuntTrick._active then return end local index = HuntTrick._index local keyword = HuntTrick._keyword local auto_go = HuntTrick._auto_go local chain = HuntTrick._chain_on_complete local target = State.get_target() HuntTrick.reset() if not chain then Util.info("Mob is here!") return end if target then QuickWhere.start_exact(index, keyword, target.mob, auto_go) else Util.info("Mob is here! (no target set for room lookup)") end end
--- Unable to hunt: chain to QW exact (skipped when chain disabled â xcp already has goto_list) -- Tested by: on_ht_unable.chains_to_qw_exact, on_ht_unable.no_target_notifies, on_ht_unable.no_chain function on_ht_unable(name, line, wildcards) if not HuntTrick._active then return end local index = HuntTrick._index local keyword = HuntTrick._keyword local auto_go = HuntTrick._auto_go local chain = HuntTrick._chain_on_complete local target = State.get_target() HuntTrick.reset() if not chain then Util.info("Hunt trick complete.") return end if target then QuickWhere.start_exact(index, keyword, target.mob, auto_go) else Util.info("Hunt trick complete. (no target set for room lookup)") end end
--- No one in area by that name: fallback to QW if first target (skipped when chain disabled) -- Tested by: on_ht_not_found.first_target_fallback, .not_first_no_qw, on_ht_not_found.no_chain function on_ht_not_found(name, line, wildcards) if not HuntTrick._active then return end local first = HuntTrick._first_target local keyword = HuntTrick._keyword local auto_go = HuntTrick._auto_go local chain = HuntTrick._chain_on_complete local target = State.get_target() HuntTrick.reset() if not chain then Util.info("Hunt trick failed.") return end if first and target then Util.info("Hunt trick failed. Attempting quick where.") QuickWhere.start_exact(1, keyword, target.mob, auto_go) else Util.info("Hunt trick failed.") end end
--- Fighting/sitting/resting: abort -- Tested by: on_ht_abort.resets function on_ht_abort(name, line, wildcards) HuntTrick.reset() Util.info("Hunt trick aborted.") end
-- AUTO HUNT triggers
--- Direction found: move in that direction, then hunt again -- wildcards[1..5] have direction from each alternation branch (only one is non-false) -- Tested by: on_ah_direction.moves_and_hunts, .opens_door, .second_capture_group, .inactive_ignored function on_ah_direction(name, line, wildcards) if not AutoHunt._active then return end
-- Extract direction from first non-false capture group local direction = nil for i = 1, 5 do local w = wildcards[i] if w and w ~= false and w ~= "" then direction = w break end end if not direction then return end
local short_dir = DIR_MAP[direction] or direction AutoHunt._direction = short_dir
-- Check GMCP exits: if exit not listed, try opening a door local room = State.get_room() if room and room.exits and not room.exits[short_dir] then Send("open " .. short_dir) end
-- Move and hunt again Send(short_dir) Send("hunt " .. AutoHunt._keyword) end
--- Mob is here: hunt complete -- Tested by: on_ah_here.completes function on_ah_here(name, line, wildcards) AutoHunt.reset() Util.info("Auto-hunt complete.") end
--- Not found or unable to hunt: abort -- Tested by: on_ah_not_found.aborts function on_ah_not_found(name, line, wildcards) AutoHunt.reset() Util.info("Auto-hunt: mob not found.") end
-- QUICK WHERE triggers
--- Where result matched: check if it's our mob, search rooms or retry -- wildcards[1] = 30-char padded mob name, wildcards[2] = room name -- On match: build goto_list from mapper, display, auto-navigate ONLY if exactly 1 room -- Tested by: on_qw_match.exact_match_searches_rooms, .keyword_match_searches_rooms, -- .no_match_increments, .max_100_stops, .auto_go_navigates, .no_auto_go_displays function on_qw_match(name, line, wildcards) if not QuickWhere._active then return end local mob_field = wildcards[1] or "" local room_name = wildcards[2] or ""
local matched = QuickWhere.check_match( mob_field, QuickWhere._exact, QuickWhere._mob_name, QuickWhere._keyword )
if not matched then -- Not our mob â increment and retry QuickWhere._index = QuickWhere._index + 1 if QuickWhere._index > 100 then Util.info("Quick where: too many retries, stopping.") QuickWhere.reset() return end SendNoEcho(string.format("where %d.%s", QuickWhere._index, QuickWhere._keyword)) return end
-- Match found â search mapper DB for rooms with this name in the area local area_key = State._room and State._room.arid or "" local target = State.get_target() QuickWhere.reset()
if area_key == "" then Util.error("Quick where: cannot search rooms â current area unknown.") return end
local results = Nav.search_rooms(room_name, area_key) Nav.build_goto_list(results)
if #Nav._goto_list > 0 then Nav.display_goto_list(target) -- Auto-navigate only when there's a single unambiguous match if #Nav._goto_list == 1 then Nav.goto_next() end else Util.info("No rooms found matching '", room_name, "' in ", area_key) end end
--- Where found nothing: reset and notify -- Tested by: on_qw_no_match.resets function on_qw_no_match(name, line, wildcards) QuickWhere.reset() Util.info("Quick where: mob not found in this area.") end
-- KILL DETECT triggers function on_kill_detect_xp(name, line, wildcards) end
-- NOEXP triggers function on_noexp_level(name, line, wildcards) end function on_noexp_powerup(name, line, wildcards) end
-- SCAN/ROOMCHARS callbacks (Item A2: parse; A5 will add render in on_scan_end)
--- {scan} marker: clear accumulator and enable content triggers -- Tested by: on_scan_start.clears_and_enables function on_scan_start(name, line, wildcards) SmartScan.reset() EnableTriggerGroup("grp_scan", true) end
--- {/scan} marker: disable content triggers, render accumulator, -- and (if smart-scan) auto-fallback to `con` for unscanned/noscan mobs. -- Tested by: on_scan_end.disables_group, on_scan_end.renders_blocks, -- on_scan_end.smart_fallback_to_con, on_scan_end.no_fallback_when_not_smart function on_scan_end(name, line, wildcards) EnableTriggerGroup("grp_scan", false)
Display.render_scan(SmartScan._scan_full_display)
-- Persist current-room scanned mobs to the SnD mobs DB so the DB-first xcp -- flow (Phase 3) can find them later. Crowley's write_mob_list_to_db. local room = State.get_room() if room and room.rmid and room.rmid > 0 and room.arid and room.arid ~= "" then for _, mob in ipairs(SmartScan._scanned_mobs) do DB.record_mob(mob, room.name or "", room.rmid, room.arid) end end
-- Smart-scan auto-con fallback: when smart-scan was running and the room had -- more mobs than scan revealed (noscan/hidden), run consider to identify them. if SmartScan._running_smart_scan and SmartScan._mob_count_here > #SmartScan._scanned_mobs then Util.info("Smart-scan: ", tostring(SmartScan._mob_count_here - #SmartScan._scanned_mobs), " unscanned mob(s); running consider...") SmartScan._con_after_scan = true SendNoEcho("con") end SmartScan._running_smart_scan = false end
--- {roomchars} marker: reset count and enable line trigger -- Tested by: on_roomchars_start.resets_and_enables function on_roomchars_start(name, line, wildcards) SmartScan._mob_count_here = 0 EnableTriggerGroup("grp_roomchars", true) end
--- {/roomchars} marker: disable line trigger -- Tested by: on_roomchars_end.disables_group function on_roomchars_end(name, line, wildcards) EnableTriggerGroup("grp_roomchars", false) end
--- Each line within {roomchars}...{/roomchars} = one mob present -- Tested by: on_roomchars_line.increments function on_roomchars_line(name, line, wildcards) SmartScan._mob_count_here = SmartScan._mob_count_here + 1 end
--- "Right here you see:" â start a new room block (current room) -- Tested by: on_scan_room_current.starts_block function on_scan_room_current(name, line, wildcards) SmartScan._scanning_current_room = true table.insert(SmartScan._scan_full_display, { header = line, distance = 0, direction = "here", mobs = {}, doors = {}, }) end
--- "(N )?Direction from here you see:" â start a new room block (nearby) -- wildcards[1] = optional "<n> " distance prefix, wildcards[2] = direction -- Tested by: on_scan_room_nearby.starts_block, _captures_distance function on_scan_room_nearby(name, line, wildcards) SmartScan._scanning_current_room = false local dist_str = wildcards[1] or "" local distance = tonumber(string.match(dist_str, "(%d)")) or 1 table.insert(SmartScan._scan_full_display, { header = line, distance = distance, direction = string.lower(wildcards[2] or ""), mobs = {}, doors = {}, }) end
--- " - <flags> <mob>" â accumulate mob into current room (skip players) -- wildcards[1] = flags string, wildcards[2] = mob name -- Tested by: on_scan_mob.appends, _skips_player_P, _skips_player_word, _no_room_block_safe function on_scan_mob(name, line, wildcards) if SmartScan.is_player(wildcards[1] or "") then return end local mob = Util.trim(wildcards[2] or "") if mob == "" then return end
local block = SmartScan._scan_full_display[#SmartScan._scan_full_display] if not block then return end -- defensive: mob line before any room header
table.insert(block.mobs, {flags = wildcards[1] or "", mob = mob, line = line}) if SmartScan._scanning_current_room then table.insert(SmartScan._scanned_mobs, string.lower(mob)) end end
--- "You see ... ." â accumulate door/feature into current room -- Tested by: on_scan_door.appends function on_scan_door(name, line, wildcards) local block = SmartScan._scan_full_display[#SmartScan._scan_full_display] if not block then return end table.insert(block.doors, {line = line}) end
--- "Nothing to see around here..." â empty scan (suppressed during smart-scan) -- Tested by: on_scan_empty.notes_unless_smart function on_scan_empty(name, line, wildcards) if not SmartScan._running_smart_scan then Note(line) end end
--- Consider outcome trigger callback â re-render mob line with [CP] tag + difficulty -- @param name trigger name (trg_con_1..trg_con_13) used to look up CON_DETAILS -- @param wildcards[1] mob name captured by the trigger pattern -- Behavior: -- - When display_overwrite is "off": re-emit the original line untouched (gag is XML-side) -- - When display_overwrite is "on" (default): emit "[CP] mob (level_range)" with colored markup -- Tracks mob in SmartScan._considered_mobs (lowercase) for smart-scan dedup. -- Tested by: on_consider_mob.renders_with_tag, _no_tag, _overwrite_off_passthrough, -- _tracks_in_considered_mobs, _unknown_trigger_safe function on_consider_mob(name, line, wildcards) local details = SmartScan.CON_DETAILS[name] if not details then return end -- defensive: unknown trigger name
local mob = Util.trim(wildcards[1] or "") if mob == "" then return end
table.insert(SmartScan._considered_mobs, string.lower(mob))
local overwrite = Config.get("display_overwrite") if overwrite ~= "on" then Note(line) return end
local tags = Display.tag_for_mob(mob)
-- During con_after_scan auto-fallback, only render tagged (target) mobs. -- Non-target mobs were already shown by scan; suppress to avoid spam. if SmartScan._con_after_scan and #tags == 0 then return end
-- Re-render: [CP] mob (level_range) with mob in silver, level in difficulty colour for _, tag in ipairs(tags) do ColourTell(tag.colour, "", tag.text) end ColourTell("silver", "", mob) ColourNote(details.colour, "", string.format(" (%s)", details.level_range)) end
